courses.title: Introduction to Asian Civilizations: History of Middle East

notes: Demonstrated proficiency equivalent to level 3 at UCLA in one modern Middle Eastern language (Arabic, Armenian, Hebrew, Persian, Turkish) or through a departmental language placement examination is required.

The Middle Eastern Studies major provides an interdisciplinary approach to studying the history, culture, politics, geography, economics, language, and philosophy of the region. The major covers a range of nations from Morocco through Iran and creates a framework for developing different perspectives on the regions complex past to its equally complex present. Listed below are the lower division preparation courses for the major. To be considered for this major, you must complete the following courses by the end of spring before transfer: One year of college level modern Middle Eastern language OR At least one year of high school in which the primary language of instruction was a modern Middle Eastern language. All courses must be taken for a letter grade. For more information regarding this major and UCLA's transfer selection process, visit https://nelc.ucla.edu and https:// admission.ucla .edu . PLEASE NOTE: The community college courses listed below have been approved to satisfy the preparation requirements for this major at UCLA, but they may not be exact equivalents of the UCLA courses listed.
